answer: Division (/) always returns a float. To do floor division and get an integer result, you can use the // operator; to calculate the remainder, you can use the % operator

Attempting to use an index that is too large will result in an "IndexError: string index out of range" error when used for indexing, whereas out-of-range slice indexes are handled gracefully when used for slicing, returning the corresponding substring or an empty string if the start index is beyond the end of the string.
6, int, float, Decimal, Fraction, complex numbers

When you assign a list to another variable, the variable refers to the existing list. Any changes you make to the list through one variable will be seen through all other variables that refer to it. To create a separate copy of the list, you can use slice operations, such as `correct_rgba = rgba[:]`, which returns a new list containing the requested elements, allowing you to modify the copy without affecting the original list.
